allies.rationale_text: Empathy helps active-listening capture not just the facts but the felt reality that makes the message meaningful.
allies.source_quote: Active listening involves paying attention to both the factual content and the emotional content.

allies.rationale_text: Critical-thinking turns active-listening into better judgment by using the gathered data to separate facts, assumptions, and interpretations.
allies.source_quote: Active listening gathers the raw, factual, and reliable data necessary for the frontal cortex to perform this logical, analytical work.

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias blocks active-listening when incoming information is filtered to support what the listener already wants to believe.
Optimization-theory can work against active-listening when pure metric logic suppresses the trust-building dialogue needed for alignment.
antagonists.source_quote: active-listening conflicts with optimization-theory when pure metric optimization suppresses trust-building dialogue required for alignment.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Active-listening conflicts with prisoners-dilemma when adversarial incentives reward information withholding over truthful sharing.
structured_tensions.source_quote: active-listening vs prisoners-dilemma: active-listening conflicts with prisoners-dilemma when adversarial incentives reward information withholding over truthful sharing.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Active-listening conflicts with optimization-theory when pure metric optimization suppresses trust-building dialogue required for alignment.
structured_tensions.source_quote: active-listening vs optimization-theory: active-listening conflicts with optimization-theory when pure metric optimization suppresses trust-building dialogue required for alignment.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Active-listening conflicts with confirmation-bias when the listener processes incoming information to confirm existing assumptions rather than genuinely absorbing the speaker's meaning.
structured_tensions.source_quote: active-listening vs confirmation-bias: active-listening conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when the listener processes incoming information to confirm existing assumptions rather than genuinely absorbing the speaker's meaning.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strongest on empathic and analytical allies plus three explicit current-vocabulary tension blocks.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor communication relation sprawl beyond the clean empathy, scrutiny, and explicit tension surface.
open_questions: Authenticity is source-real ally pressure too, but the retained set favors the stronger listening-to-judgment bridge and explicit tensions.
